pattern: Scanning once for the first and last index whose value is prime gives the largest index gap between primes.
hints: The answer only depends on where primes sit, not on the non-prime values.
The widest gap is always between the earliest prime and the latest prime in the array.
Record the first and last index whose value is prime, then return their difference.

edge_inputs.description: 1 is not prime so it must be ignored
smallest prime is 2 and largest value 100 is not prime

stress.description: random values in range with at least one guaranteed prime
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    arr[rng.randrange(n)] = 2
    return [arr]
stress.note: force a prime so the input is always valid
